evals.prompt: /ds-trace start "churn analysis for Q1 enterprise segment"
evals.expected_output: Start mode creates a trace file in traces/ directory with correct YAML frontmatter, phase scaffolding, and Session Totals section. Confirms tracing is active to the user.
expectations: A trace file is created at traces/trace-YYYY-MM-DD-churn-analysis-for-q1-enterprise-segment.md (or similar kebab-case slug)
The trace file starts with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines
YAML frontmatter contains 'session_id' field with today's date and topic slug
YAML frontmatter contains 'date' field with today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format
YAML frontmatter contains 'objective' field with the topic text
YAML frontmatter contains 'status: in-progress'
YAML frontmatter contains numeric fields: total_duration_min, total_tokens, total_cost_usd, steps_success, steps_error, files_read, files_written
YAML frontmatter contains 'tools_summary' field
The trace file contains a top-level heading '# Trace:' with the topic name
The trace file contains at least 3 phase headers (## Phase:)
The trace file contains a '## 📊 Session Totals' section
The confirmation message to the user mentions the trace file path
The confirmation message mentions what will be captured (decisions, tools, timing)

evals.expected_output: Reflect mode reads the trace, extracts learnings grouped by target (CLAUDE.md, learnings.md, memory), presents them for approval with session health metrics, and waits for DS confirmation before writing.
expectations: The reflect output includes a 'Reflection Summary' or equivalent heading
Learnings are grouped by target: CLAUDE.md, traces/learnings.md, and Memory
Each proposed learning includes the target destination (where it will be written)
Each proposed learning includes the proposed text that will be written
Each proposed learning references which trace entry it came from (source)
A session health summary is included with at least: duration, error ratio
The output asks for DS approval before writing any learnings (gate)
CLAUDE.md learnings are formatted as actionable rules with dates
Learnings file patterns include a Phase field and a Why field
Memory entries include project-specific details (dataset/table/column names)
The reflect step suggests next actions (e.g., run /ds-review)

evals.expected_output: When no trace file exists, the reflect mode gracefully offers a retrospective trace option instead of failing. If the user declines, it suggests using /ds-trace start next time.
expectations: Output acknowledges that no trace file was found
Output offers to create a retrospective trace from session memory
If retrospective is accepted: a trace file is created with a disclaimer about estimated data
The disclaimer mentions that timing and token counts are estimated
If retrospective is declined: output suggests using /ds-trace start next time
The fallback does NOT produce an error or crash — it handles gracefully
